name: Authentic Carolina Pork Barbeque
recipeIngredient: 1 ¼ oz Crushed red pepper flakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all sauce ingredients together.
2. Use the mixture as a basting sauce for the meat.
The pork must be barbecued, which means it should be cooked slowly and long over an actual wood fire, preferably hickory. The temperature should be around 220 degrees Fahrenheit, and it takes at least 1-½ hours per pound or until the internal temperature reaches 150-160 degrees Fahrenheit. This can be challenging to achieve in a typical backyard Weber kettle, although it is possible. It must be served on a Cheap White Bun (CWB). After that, the only question is whether to serve it with or without sweet cole slaw on top.
